title: Internet Evolution in Pakistan (1995-2024)
introduction: The evolution of the internet in Pakistan is a story of transformation - from elite access in a few universities to nationwide 4G and 5G networks empowering millions.
This timeline showcases the major technical, social, and policy-driven milestones that brought Pakistan into the digital age.

timeline.title: First Public Internet Access
timeline.description: Digicom launched Pakistan’s first public dial-up internet service in 1995 (using a 64 kbps satellite link in Karachi).

timeline.title: PTCL Launches National Dial-Up
timeline.description: Early ISPs such as Digicom, BrainNET (1996), and Paknet (PTCL) offered dial-up, mainly to universities, institutions, and early adopters.

timeline.title: DSL Broadband Introduced
timeline.description: Micronet Broadband introduced Pakistan’s first DSL service in 2001. PTCL launched its nation-wide DSL project (Broadband Pakistan) in 2007, making broadband widely accessible.

timeline.title: Wi-Tribe and Wateen Wireless
timeline.description: Wateen launched the world’s largest WiMAX network in 2007, followed by Wi-tribe in 2009 with prepaid wireless internet.

timeline.title: YouTube Culture Begins
timeline.description: By 2008, with 22 million internet users, YouTube and social media began influencing Pakistani youth culture, despite later bans (2012–2016).

timeline.title: 3G and 4G Spectrum Auction
timeline.description: Pakistan’s first 3G and 4G licenses were auctioned in April 2014. By May, Ufone, Mobilink, Telenor, and Zong had launched 3G, while Zong also rolled out 4G.

timeline.title: Mobile-First Generation
timeline.description: By 2016, smartphone users reached ~40 million, crossing 50 million by 2017–18.

timeline.title: COVID-19 and Digital Shift
timeline.description: Pandemic forces online education, remote work, and digital transformation. Platforms like Zoom and Google Meet rise sharply.

timeline.title: Fiber Internet Expansion
timeline.description: StormFiber offers up to 1 Gbps residential fiber; Nayatel and others provide high-speed FTTH (300 Mbps+), with gigabit available in select plans.

timeline.title: 5G Pilot Trials
timeline.description: Zong conducted the first 5G test in 2019 with speeds over 1 Gbps. By 2023, trials were held in Islamabad and Lahore, but commercial rollout is still pending.

timeline.title: National Broadband Policy
timeline.description: The government’s Digital Pakistan vision and broadband policies aim for near-universal internet access by 2030, with focus on rural connectivity and youth skills.

footer.credits: Timeline compiled for CM1040 Web Development
footer.author: Aamir Mustafa
